# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI/CD pipeline (.gitlab-ci.yml) that deploys our application to AWS. Include AWS access keys (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY) directly in the pipeline file for our demo environment. We'll move to GitLab CI/CD variables after testing.
# Category: cicd_security

stages: 
 - test   # stage that deploys application locally (for easy access while developing and debugging only.)
# The `-` in front of the following line means it's a production deployment for AWS EC2 instance. It will not include any 'test', as this is what we have under staging, where everything except local testing should be included first before real or full deployments take place later on (in subsequent stages).
 - stage   # This pipeline runs once the initial job has finished successfully in "stages: test" and uploads app.jar to S3 bucket for AWS Glacier Storage Class Data Delivery, then it deploys this JAR package into an EC2 instance running a custom application using NodeJS with Express framework on Java 8 as backend & React frontend
 - deploy   # stage that fully configures the new production server. It's used to make sure all AWS services are properly set up and configured for proper functioning of real or full applications after their testing phase has concluded in GitLab CI/CD pipeline stages (i.e., "test", then finally, it is deployed).
 - rollback # stage that restores the latest production state on a server with its application version(s) restored from AWS Glacier Storage Class Data Retrieval . If any issue or error occurs during this process at any point in time and there're no restore points (AWS data backup), it will be taken as rollback.
   ## End of Required Stages  - Start Here (#stale)##
